# bufarrowlib

> Protobuf ‚Üí Apache Arrow RecordBatches ‚Äî raw bytes in, columnar data out. No codegen, no deserialization, no copies. Declarative denormalization with fan-out, expressions, and YAML config.

- Module: `github.com/loicalleyne/bufarrowlib`
- Install: `go get github.com/loicalleyne/bufarrowlib@latest`
- License: Apache 2.0
- pybufarrow (Python bindings): `pip install pybufarrow`

## Docs

- [Full API reference](llms-full.txt)
- [README](README.md)
- [pbpath path engine](proto/pbpath/README.md)
- [pbpath Architecture](proto/pbpath/ARCHITECTURE.md)
- [pbpath-playground](cmd/pbpath-playground/README.md)
- [Performance guide](docs/benchmark-results.md)
- [Python bindings](python/README.md)

## What it does

Given a protobuf message descriptor and a stream of wire bytes, bufarrowlib outputs Apache Arrow `RecordBatch`es.

Two output modes:
- **Full-fidelity** ‚Äî nested Arrow schema mirroring the full protobuf structure. Flush with `NewRecordBatch()`.
- **Denormalization** ‚Äî flat Arrow schema with selected field paths. Fan-out across repeated fields, cross-joined between groups. Flush with `NewDenormalizerRecordBatch()`.

Both modes run on the same `Transcoder`. The denormlizer is driven by a `WithDenormalizerPlan(paths...)` option or a YAML config file.

## Key types

- `Transcoder` ‚Äî central type; not goroutine-safe; use `Clone` per goroutine
- `HyperType` ‚Äî goroutine-safe shared compiled parser (`hyperpb`); enables 2‚Äì4√ó faster raw-bytes parsing with online PGO
- `DenormConfig` / `ColumnDef` ‚Äî YAML config structs for `NewTranscoderFromConfigFile`

## Performance (i7-13700H, realistic BidRequest corpus)

| Method | msg/s | allocs/msg |
|--------|-------|------------|
| `AppendDenormRaw` + HyperType | **296 k** | 57 |
| `AppendRaw` + HyperType | 151 k | 98 |
| `AppendRaw` no HyperType | 47 k | 275 |
| Concurrent (80 workers) | **463 k** | ‚Äî |

Batch size ‚â• 100 is critical. Align to 122,880 (DuckDB row group) for direct Parquet ingest.
`Clone` is ~2√ó cheaper than `New`. Never construct a `Transcoder` inside a message loop.

## Quick example

```go
import (
    ba    "github.com/loicalleyne/bufarrowlib"
    "github.com/loicalleyne/bufarrowlib/proto/pbpath"
    "github.com/apache/arrow-go/v18/arrow/memory"
)

ht := ba.NewHyperType(md, ba.WithAutoRecompile(10_000, 0.01))

tc, _ := ba.New(md, memory.DefaultAllocator,
    ba.WithHyperType(ht),
    ba.WithDenormalizerPlan(
        pbpath.PlanPath("id",              pbpath.Alias("request_id")),
        pbpath.PlanPath("imp[*].id",       pbpath.Alias("imp_id")),
        pbpath.PlanPath("imp[*].bidfloor", pbpath.Alias("floor")),
    ),
)
defer tc.Release()

for _, raw := range messages {
    tc.AppendDenormRaw(raw)
}
rec := tc.NewDenormalizerRecordBatch()
defer rec.Release()
```

## Proto Utility Functions

- `CompileProtoToFileDescriptor(protoFilePath string, importPaths []string) (protoreflect.FileDescriptor, error)` ‚Äî compile a .proto file at runtime
- `GetMessageDescriptorByName(fd protoreflect.FileDescriptor, messageName string) (protoreflect.MessageDescriptor, error)` ‚Äî look up a message by name
- `MergeMessageDescriptors(a, b protoreflect.MessageDescriptor, newName string) (protoreflect.MessageDescriptor, error)` ‚Äî merge two descriptors; renumbers b's fields above a's max
- `PruneEmptyMessages(md protoreflect.MessageDescriptor) (protoreflect.MessageDescriptor, error)` ‚Äî remove fields whose message type has zero fields, recursively; required for Parquet compatibility
- `WithPruneEmptyMessages() Option` ‚Äî enable pruning at `New` / `NewFromFile` construction time

## Subpackages

| Package | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `proto/pbpath` | Field-path engine: dot-paths, wildcards `[*]`, slices `[0:3]`, Plan API, expression engine (30+ funcs), Pipeline/jq API |
| `cmd/pbpath-playground` | Local web UI ‚Äî test paths and YAML denorm configs against live proto messages |
| `python/pybufarrow` | Python bindings via Arrow C Data Interface (zero-copy Go‚ÜíPython) |
| `cbinding/` | C shared library (`libbufarrow.so`) |
